item.text: {"case_id":"mtj-039","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":false,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The response calibrates the abstract question to the actual decision context: a new graduate choosing between two offers. It gives situated offer-selection criteria around growth density, normalized overtime, compensation, personal goals, health, and manager/team quality, while preserving factual/legal-health layers such as overtime pay, time off, and physical sustainability. It avoids generic moralizing and commits to a usable decision rule: choose the offer with higher growth certainty, reliable manager, rising business, and bounded overtime."}
